title: grassy green german curds gratin
description: a light and airy german dish featuring cheese curds, perfect for a buddhist-inspired meal prep
instructions: in a medium saucepan, bring a quart of water to a boil.
add the gruyère cheese curds to the boiling water and cook for 3-5 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
using a slotted spoon, transfer the curds to a colander and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process.
in a large mixing bowl, combine the chopped parsley, dill, and green onions.
add the cooked cheese curds to the bowl and stir until they are evenly coated with the herb mixture.
in a separate bowl, mix together the beaten eggs and melted butter.
add the flour to the egg mixture and stir until smooth.
in a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, arrange a layer of the curd and herb mixture.
brush the top of the layer with the egg wash and sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
repeat the layering process until all of the ingredients have been used, finishing with a layer of egg wash on top.
sprinkle the top of the gratin with chopped fresh chives and a pinch of salt.
cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the eggs are set.
